note: Conservative rest-timer pose suggestions. Prefer gentle, short, low-risk positions. Do not require deep stretching during short strength-training rests.

leg-extension.reason: Gently opens the anterior hip and thigh after quad-dominant work.
Targets the quadriceps, but use only if knees tolerate it.

rdl.reason: Gently lengthens posterior chain without forcing end range.
Targets hamstrings while keeping the spine supported.

romanian-deadlift.reason: Gently lengthens posterior chain without forcing end range.

hip-thrust.reason: Opens hip flexors after repeated hip extension.
Targets glute and external rotator tension.

lat-pulldown.reason: Opens lats and side body after vertical pulling.

row.reason: Gently rotates the thoracic spine and opens posterior shoulder.

chest-press.reason: Opens the chest and anterior shoulder after pressing.

bench-press.reason: Opens the chest and anterior shoulder after pressing.

shoulder-press.reason: Lets the shoulders flex overhead gently while breathing.

bicep-curl.reason: Gently opens the front of the arm and forearm.

tricep-extension.reason: Matches the tissue just trained while staying simple for a rest interval.

calf-raise.reason: Alternates gentle calf lengthening without long static holds.

squat.reason: Rehearses squat mobility and breathing at low intensity.
